intent: In DATA_DIR there is a 10x matrix triplet for one PBMC sample — files prefixed GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1. Run a standard Seurat workflow in R: load, QC, normalize, PCA, neighbors, clustering, UMAP, and FindAllMarkers; show me a UMAP and the top markers per cluster.

blocks.text: Good — I can see the 10x matrix triplet for GSM5746268 (the sample you requested), plus two others. Let me now read the Seurat recipe to understand the exact workflow and best practices.

blocks.content: {"status": "ok", "name": "seurat-scrna", "description": "Authoritative single-cell RNA-seq QC + clustering + cluster annotation with R/Seurat (v5).", "body": "scRNA-seq QC + clustering + annotation with R/Seurat v5 (standard PBMC3k workflow). Provision: ensure_capability('Seurat'), then library(Seurat). THE THREE CHOICES THAT DEFINE THE RESULT \u2014 surface them with present_plan before committing: (1) QC thresholds (subset cutoffs on nFeature_RNA and percent.mt); (2) number of PCs/dims (read off the ElbowPlot); (3) clustering resolution (FindClusters). Steps: ReadMtx -> CreateSeuratObject(min.cells=3,min.features=200) -> PercentageFeatureSet('^MT-') -> subset QC -> NormalizeData -> FindVariableFeatures -> ScaleData -> RunPCA -> FindNeighbors -> FindClusters -> RunUMAP -> JoinLayers -> FindAllMarkers. Load ggplot2 and dplyr explicitly; never library(tidyverse)."}

blocks.text: Now let me present the plan. The recipe specifies three key choices: QC thresholds, number of PCs, and clustering resolution. I'll propose reasonable defaults and we can adjust if needed.

input.title: Seurat workflow for GSM5746268
input.summary: Load 10x matrix triplet → QC → normalize → PCA → neighbors → clustering → UMAP → FindAllMarkers; display UMAP and top markers per cluster.

steps.title: Load 10x matrix triplet (MTX format)
steps.description: Read GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1 barcodes, features, and matrix files using Seurat::ReadMtx and CreateSeuratObject with standard filtering (min.cells=3, min.features=200).

steps.title: QC: compute metrics and filter
steps.description: Calculate nFeature_RNA, nCount_RNA, and percent.mt (mitochondrial %). Remove cells with <200 or >5000 features, and <5% or >20% mitochondrial content. Adjust thresholds if the user specifies.

steps.title: Normalize and find highly variable genes
Scale and run PCA
steps.description: ScaleData → RunPCA (npcs=50). User will review an ElbowPlot to confirm number of PCs for downstream (default: use top 30).

steps.title: Find neighbors, cluster, and run UMAP
steps.description: FindNeighbors (dims=1:30) → FindClusters (resolution=0.8, default; adjust if needed) → RunUMAP (dims=1:30).

steps.title: Differential expression: FindAllMarkers
steps.description: JoinLayers, then FindAllMarkers (only.pos=TRUE, min.pct=0.1, logfc.threshold=0.25) to identify marker genes for each cluster.

steps.title: Visualize: UMAP and top markers table
steps.description: Plot UMAP colored by cluster; tabulate top 5 marker genes per cluster (sorted by avg_log2FC).

assumptions: Data is PBMC 10x GSM5746268 (mouse, based on the filename)
Mitochondrial genes are prefixed 'mt-' (standard mouse convention; will be detected from features file)
QC thresholds: nFeature 200–5000, percent.mt 5–20% (typical PBMC ranges; adjust if needed)
Resolution 0.8 for clustering (moderate granularity; can tune after seeing initial clusters)
Top 30 PCs for downstream (will confirm via elbow plot)
